notes: Auto-executed tool call, ungated: the user asks codex to run a quick read-only shell command (pwd) and report the directory. Codex runs the tool autonomously inside its sandbox with NO approval gate (capabilities.json permission_hooks:false), so the canonical arc is ready→working→ready and state NEVER enters waiting. This cell spotlights the auto-executed (ungated) nature; the sibling full-lifecycle-toolcall (same coverage_id) already exercised the function_call/function_call_output round-trip mid-turn. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver, not headless `codex exec`: a single-shot `exec` turn is too short for the daemon to observe the ready→working flip (debounce coalesces it — basic-turn headless turn_start fails 3/5) and its turn-end surfaces as process_exited rather than a working→ready settle. The interactive REPL stays alive across the turn, so the daemon observes the full working→ready arc on the live rollout session, then process_exited only at REPL teardown (same shape as multi-turn-conversation 10/10 and the sibling 6/6). The tool call itself is observable in transcript.jsonl as codex function_call / function_call_output events (parser.go); the phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the auto-executed tool call is asserted via the single uninterrupted working span it sustains plus the no-waiting invariant, not a dedicated phase.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt requiring a tool call → working on the rollout session
Tool auto-executes (no approval gate) and the turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
